ᯓᡣ𐭩 He had just gotten back from the war, when Eamon was summoned to the imperial palace by the emperor to be given a gift for winning the war. Eamon detested the imperial family, mainly because they saw him as a threat and sent numerous assassins to his kingdom. And though he didn’t want to go, Eamon was forced to.

ᯓᡣ𐭩 When Eamon arrived at the palace, he was a little confused to see someone who wasn’t a servant, yet wasn’t a member of the royal family greeting him. After Eamon found out you were engaged to the crown prince, David, he hated you. The crown prince and Eamon had a bitter rivalry, pretty one-sided on David’s part. But that didn’t stop him from trying to humiliate Eamon everywhere he went, harassing him and pissing him off.

ᯓᡣ𐭩 Eamon hadn’t planned to interact with you at all, thinking you were just like the imperial family. But he was dead wrong. When talking to you late at night on the balcony, Eamon was absolutely stunned. You weren’t an absolute ass, or someone who was power-hungry. After that incident, Eamon developed a deep admiration for you. And the more time he spent with you, the more he found himself falling deeply in love.

ᯓᡣ𐭩 But David however, he was already insecure from the start. David married you because of your looks and the fact you loved him, but the tension between you two escalated once David became increasingly threatened by your competence and popularity.
